text: <sup>{The Book of the Prophet}</sup> <sup>{Habakkuk}</sup> d <sup>{Habakkuk's Oracle}</sup> d The pronouncement <sup>{Or revelation}</sup> that the prophet Habakkuk <sup>{The Heb. name Habakkuk means embrace}</sup> perceived.
<sup>{The Prophet's First Complaint}</sup> d "How long, Lord , must I cry out for help, but you won't listen? I'm crying out to you, «Oppression!'but you aren't providing deliverance.
Why are you forcing me to look at iniquity and to stare at wickedness? Social havoc and oppression are all around me; there are legal conflicts, and disputes abound.

Therefore, the Law has become paralyzed, and justice never comes about. Because criminals outnumber <sup>{Lit. are surrounding}</sup> the righteous, whenever judgments are issued, they come out crooked."

<sup>{God's Response: The Coming Chaldean Invasion}</sup> d "Look out at the nations and pay attention! Be astounded! Be really astounded! Because something is happening in your lifetime that you won't believe, even if it were described down to the smallest detail. <sup>{The Heb. lacks down to the smallest detail}</sup>

Watch out! For I am bringing in the Chaldeans, <sup>{I.e. Babylonian invaders}</sup> that cruel and impetuous <sup>{Or rash}</sup> people, who sweep across the earth dispossessing people <sup>{The Heb. lacks people}</sup> from homes not their own.
They are terrible and fearsome; their brand of justice and sense of honor derive only from themselves!

Their horses are swifter than leopards, and more cunning than wolves that attack at night. Their horsemen are galloping as they approach from far away. They swoop in like ravenous vultures. <sup>{Or eagles}</sup>
"They all come to oppress hordes of them, their faces pressing onward they take prisoners as numerous as <sup>{The Heb. lacks as numerous as}</sup> the desert sand!
They make fun of kings, deriding those who rule. They laugh at all of the fortified places, constructing ramps to seize them.
Then like <sup>{The Heb. lacks like}</sup> the wind sweeping by they will pass through they're guilty because they say <sup>{The Heb. lacks they say}</sup> their power is their god."

<sup>{The Prophet's Second Complaint}</sup> d "Haven't you existed forever, Lord my God, my Holy One? We won't die! Lord , you've prepared them <sup>{I.e. the Babylonian invaders}</sup> for judgment; Rock, you've sentenced them <sup>{I.e. the Babylonian invaders}</sup> to correction.

Your eyes are too pure to gaze upon evil; and you cannot tolerate wickedness. So why do you tolerate the treacherous? And why do you stay silent while the wicked devour those who are more righteous than they are?
"You have fashioned mankind like fish in the ocean, like creeping things that have no ruler.

The adversary <sup>{I.e. the Babylonian invaders}</sup> captures them with a hook, gathering them up in a fishing net. He collects them with a dragnet, rejoicing and gloating over his catch. <sup>{The Heb. lacks over his catch}</sup>
Therefore he sacrifices to his fishing net, and burns incense in the presence of his dragnet, because by them his assets increase and he gets plenty of food.
Is he to continue to empty his fishing net? Will he ever stop killing entire <sup>{The Heb. lacks entire}</sup> nations without mercy?"

<sup>{Habakkuk Waits for God's Answer}</sup> d "I will stand at my guard post and station myself on a tower. I will wait and see what the Lord <sup>{The Heb. lacks the LORD}</sup> will say about me and what I <sup>{Syr he}</sup> will answer when he reprimands me. <sup>{Lit. answer at my reprimand}</sup> "

<sup>{God's Reply to the Prophet's Complaint}</sup> d When he answered, the Lord told me: "Write out the revelation, engraving it clearly on the tablets, so that a courier may run with it. <sup>{Or that whoever reads it may run}</sup>

For the revelation pertains to an appointed time it speaks truthfully <sup>{Lit. speaks without deception}</sup> about the end. Though it delays, wait for it, because it will surely come about it will not be late!
"Notice their <sup>{I.e. the Babylonian invaders}</sup> arrogance they have no inward uprightness <sup>{Lit. no uprightness of soul}</sup> but the righteous will live by their faith.

Moreover, just as wine leads astray the proud and powerful man, he <sup>{I.e. Babylonian invaders personified in their king}</sup> remains restless; he <sup>{I.e. Babylonian invaders personified in their king}</sup> has expanded his appetite like the afterlife <sup>{Lit. Sheol ; i.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> or death itself, he <sup>{I.e. Babylonian invaders personified in their king}</sup> is never satisfied. He <sup>{I.e. Babylonian invaders personified in their king}</sup> gathers to himself all of the nations, taking captive all of the people for himself."

<sup>{Judgment on the Plunderer of Nations}</sup> d "Will not all of these ridicule him with mocking scorn? They will say, «Woe to the one who hordes for himself what isn't his. How long will you enrich yourself by extortion? '<sup>{Lit. by loans}</sup>
Won't your creditors revolt unexpectedly? Won't those who make you tremble wake up? As a result, you'll become their prey!

Because you plundered many nations, all of their remnants will plunder you. Human blood has been shed, <sup>{The Heb. lacks has been shed}</sup> and violence has been done to <sup>{Lit. violence of}</sup> the land, to the city, and to all who live in it."

<sup>{Judgment on Those who Think They are Safe}</sup> d "Woe to the one who amasses profit upon unjust profit in order to establish his household, so he can establish a secure place <sup>{Lit. establish his nest}</sup> on the heights and escape from the power of evil.
You have brought shame to yourself <sup>{Lit. to your house}</sup> by killing many people you are forfeiting your own life.
Indeed, the stone will cry out from the wall and the rafter will respond from the woodwork."
<sup>{Judgment on the Lawless}</sup> d "Woe to the one who founds a city upon bloodshed, and constructs a city by lawlessness.
Is it not because of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies that people grow tired putting out fires, <sup>{Lit. tired for the sufficiency of fire}</sup> and nations weary themselves over nothing?
Indeed, the earth will be filled with knowledge of the glory of the Lord , as water fills <sup>{Lit. covers}</sup> the sea."

<sup>{Judgment on the Violent}</sup> d "Woe to the one who supplies his neighbor with a drink! You are forcing your bottle <sup>{Lit. wine skin}</sup> on him, <sup>{The Heb. lacks on him}</sup> making him drunk so you can see them naked.

You are filled with dishonor instead of glory. So go ahead, <sup>{The Heb. lacks go ahead}</sup> drink and be naked! The Lord <sup>{Lit. The power of the Lord's right hand}</sup> will turn against you, and utter disgrace will debase your reputation. <sup>{Lit. will come upon your glory}</sup>

Indeed, the violence done to Lebanon will overtake you, and the destruction of the beasts will terrorize you <sup>{The Heb. lacks you}</sup> because you shed human blood and did violence to <sup>{Lit. violence of}</sup> the land, to the city, and to all who live in it."

<sup>{Judgment on the Idol Maker}</sup> d "Where is the benefit in owning <sup>{The Heb. lacks owning}</sup> a carved image, that motivates its maker to carve <sup>{Lit. because its maker carved}</sup> it? It is only a cast image a teacher that lies because the engraver entrusts himself to his carving, crafting speechless idols.

"Woe to the one who says to a tree, «Wake up!'or «Arise!'to a speechless stone. Idols <sup>{Lit. Things}</sup> like this can't teach, can they? Look, even though it is overlaid with gold and silver, there's no breath in it at all."
<sup>{The Lord's Final Counsel to Habakkuk}</sup> d "The Lord is in his holy Temple. All the earth be quiet in his presence."
<sup>{Habakkuk's Prayer of Faith}</sup> d A prayer by the prophet Habakkuk, set to music. <sup>{Lit. prayer upon Shigionoth}</sup>

Lord , as I listen to what has been said about you, I am afraid. Lord , revive your work throughout all of our lives reveal yourself <sup>{The Heb. lacks yourself}</sup> throughout all of our lives when you <sup>{The Heb. lacks you}</sup> are angry, remember compassion.

God comes from Teman <sup>{I.e. an Edomite desert town}</sup> the Holy One from Mount Paran. <sup>{I.e. in the Sinai desert}</sup> InterludeHis glory spreads throughout the heavens, and praises about him fill the earth.
His radiance is like sunlight; beams of light shine <sup>{The Heb. lacks shine}</sup> from his hand, where his strength lays hidden.
Before him pestilence walks, and disease follows behind him. <sup>{Lit. follows at his feet}</sup>
He stood up and shook the land; with his stare he startled the nations. The age-old mountains were shattered, and the ancient hilltops bowed down. His ways are eternal.
I saw the tents of Cushan in distress, and the tent curtains of the land of Midian in anguish.

Was the Lord displeased with the rivers? Was your anger directed <sup>{The Heb. lacks directed}</sup> against the watercourses or your wrath against the sea? Indeed, you rode upon your horses, upon your chariots of deliverance.

Your bow was exposed, and your {The Heb. lacks your} arrows targeted by command.
Interlude
You split the earth with rivers.
When the mountains looked upon you, they trembled; the overflowing water passed by, the ocean shouted, and its waves <sup>{Lit. hands}</sup> surged upward.
The sun and moon stand still in their orbits; at the glint of your arrows they speed along, even at the gleam of your flashing spear.
You march through the land in righteous <sup>{The Heb. lacks righteous}</sup> indignation; you tread down the nations in anger.

You marched out to deliver your people, to deliver with your anointed. You struck the head of the house of the wicked; you stripped him naked from head to foot.
Interlude
With his own lances you pierced the heads of his warriors, who came out like a windstorm to scatter us <sup>{Lit. me}</sup> their joy is to devour the afflicted who are in hiding.
You rode on the sea with your horses, even riding <sup>{The Heb. lacks even riding}</sup> the crested waves of mighty waters.

<sup>{Habakkuk's Response}</sup> d I heard and I trembled within. My lips quivered at the noise. My legs gave way beneath me, <sup>{Lit. Rottenness enters my bones}</sup> and I trembled. Nevertheless, I await the day of distress that will dawn on our invaders.

Even though the fig tree does not blossom, and there are no grapes on the vines; even if the olive harvest fails, and the fields produce nothing edible; even if the flock is snatched from the sheepfold, and there is no herd in the stalls
as for me, I will rejoice in the Lord. I will find my joy in the God who delivers me.
The Lord God is my strength he will make my feet like those of a deer, equipping me to tread on my mountain heights.
